description_long: This is a large, rustic terracotta plant pot with a rounded, bulbous body and a wide, thick-rimmed opening at the top. Four protruding side pockets are evenly spaced around the main body, each forming a smaller planting compartment with its own open mouth. The surface is textured and slightly rough, showing natural color variations from light tan to earthy brown, with some greenish moss or weathering visible in crevices. The main opening and side pockets all contain dark soil, suggesting the pot is ready for planting or already in use. The pot's robust construction and multiple planting areas make it ideal for growing several small plants or herbs simultaneously. Its design is both functional and decorative, suitable for outdoor urban environments such as patios, sidewalks, or community gardens.
description: A large, rustic terracotta plant pot with a wide top opening and four side pockets for multiple plantings. The surface is textured and weathered, with earthy tones and visible soil in each compartment.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main opening and three of the four side pockets, with visible soil and weathered surface details.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the rounded body and the arrangement of the side pockets, with the top rim clearly visible.
description_view_2: View showing the opposite side, with all four side pockets visible and the pot's curvature emphasized.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back two side pockets and the pot's rounded form, with some greenish weathering visible.
attribute_placement_context: commonly used in community gardens or on urban patios for growing herbs or small plants
